perturbation.original_text: SMT LABS PTY LTD operates a laboratory management system in compliance with ISO/IEC 17025 and requirements as set out in the South African National Accreditation System (SANAS) policies and procedures.
perturbation.changed_text: SMT LABS PTY LTD operates a laboratory management system and generally aims to align with ISO/IEC 17025 and SANAS policies and procedures.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'generally aims to align' weakens the obligation to comply with ISO/IEC 17025 and SANAS policies, creating ambiguity. It's no longer a strict compliance requirement.
perturbation.law_explanation: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 is a quality standard. Saying they 'generally aim to align' allows them to deviate from strict adherence without clear justification, which directly contradicts the standard's core principle of consistent and reliable laboratory operations as specified in section 4.1.3
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: ISO/IEC 17025:2017 - General requirements for the competence of testing and calibration laboratories Skip to main content Reference number ISO/IEC 17025:2017 © ISO 2025 International Standard ISO/IEC 17025:2017 General requirements for the competence of testing and calibration laboratories Edition 3 2017-11 Read sample ISO/IEC 17025:2017 66912 Published (Edition 3, 2017) This publication was last reviewed and confirmed in 2023. Therefore this version remains current. ISO/IEC 17025:2017 ISO/IEC 17025:2017 66912 Language English French Russian Spanish Format PDF + ePub PDF + ePub + Redline Paper PDF + ePub Paper PDF Paper PDF Paper CHF 155 Add to cart * Shipping costs not included Convert Swiss francs (CHF) to your currency What is ISO/IEC 17025? ISO/IEC 17025 is the international standard for testing and calibration laboratories . It sets out requirements for the competence, impartiality, and consistent operation of laboratories, ensuring the accuracy and reliability of their testing and calibration results. Why is ISO/IEC 17025 important? This standard is vital for laboratories as it enhances the credibility of their testing and calibration work , fostering trust among clients and regulatory authorities. Compliance with ISO/IEC 17025 demonstrates a laboratory's commitment to quality, technical proficiency, and scientific rigor . Benefits of ISO/IEC 17025 Establishes a global benchmark for laboratory quality and reliability Enhances confidence in test and calibration results , both domestically and internationally Facilitates cooperation between laboratories and other bodies by generating wider acceptance of results Reduces the need for retesting , saving time and resources FAQ Who should implement ISO/IEC 17025? Laboratories of any size that perform testing and calibration, seeking to demonstrate their operational competence and reliability. How does ISO/IEC 17025 relate to laboratory accreditation? Accreditation bodies use ISO/IEC 17025 as the criteria for assessing and accredi
perturbation.justification: The original text states that SMT LABS PTY LTD operates in 'compliance' with ISO/IEC 17025 and SANAS policies, indicating a strict adherence. The changed text weakens this to 'generally aims to align,' introducing ambiguity and allowing for potential deviations. The ISO/IEC 17025:2017 snippet states that the standard 'sets out requirements for the competence, impartiality, and consistent operation of laboratories, ensuring the accuracy and reliability of their testing and calibration results'. By only 'aiming to align,' the changed text fails to ensure the consistent operation that the standard requires, which creates a contradiction because the original contract requires strict compliance.

perturbation.original_text: SMT LABS PTY LTD shall be responsible, through legally enforceable commitments, for the management of all information obtained or created during the performance of laboratory activities.
perturbation.changed_text: SMT LABS PTY LTD will endeavor to be responsible, through commitments, for the management of information obtained during laboratory activities.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall be responsible, through legally enforceable commitments' to 'will endeavor to be responsible, through commitments' introduces ambiguity by weakening the obligation and removing the 'legally enforceable' aspect. The original text makes clear that the commitments should be legally binding; the modified text makes the obligation weaker and the commitments may not necessarily be legally binding.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Protection of Personal Information Act, 2013 (South Africa)
perturbation.law_explanation: POPIA Section 19 requires that responsible parties implement appropriate, reasonable technical and organizational measures to ensure the integrity and confidentiality of personal information. Changing 'shall be responsible, through legally enforceable commitments' to 'will endeavor to be responsible, through commitments' introduces ambiguity and weakens the measure in contradiction with POPIA Section 19.
perturbation.justification: The original contract uses 'shall be responsible, through legally enforceable commitments,' creating a clear legal obligation. The modified version, 'will endeavor to be responsible, through commitments,' weakens this obligation and removes the 'legally enforceable' aspect, introducing ambiguity. The Perturbation Explanation highlights that the modified text makes the commitments potentially not legally binding. The Law Explanation states that this change contradicts POPIA Section 19, which requires implementing appropriate, reasonable measures to ensure information integrity and confidentiality. Although there are no law snippets, the combined explanations show the change weakens the original obligation, creating a significant contradiction with the intent of the POPIA Section 19.

perturbation.original_text: When the laboratory is required by law or authorized by contractual arrangements to release confidential information, the customer shall, unless prohibited by law, be notified of the information provided BEFORE information released.
perturbation.changed_text: When the laboratory is required by law or authorized by contractual arrangements to release confidential information, the customer should be notified when feasible.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'shall, unless prohibited by law, be notified of the information provided BEFORE information released' with 'should be notified when feasible' introduces ambiguity and weakens the obligation. The original text makes notification almost mandatory (unless legally prohibited), while the changed text makes it discretionary based on feasibility.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Protection of Personal Information Act, 2013 (South Africa)
perturbation.law_explanation: POPIA Section 22 dictates what must happen if a data breach occurs. It requires informing the data subject. Changing the text to 'should be notified when feasible' contradicts POPIA because it is not conditional; it should happen without the condition of feasibility.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall, unless prohibited by law, be notified...BEFORE information released' to 'should be notified when feasible' significantly weakens the obligation to notify the customer before releasing confidential information. The original text imposes a near-mandatory requirement (only excused when prohibited by law), whereas the changed text makes notification discretionary and dependent on feasibility. This introduces ambiguity, aligning with the Ambiguities Legal Contradiction definition, and could lead to non-compliance. According to the Law Explanation, POPIA Section 22 mandates informing the data subject in case of a data breach, and the change introduces a conditional element ('when feasible') that undermines this obligation. Although no specific law snippets were provided, the explanation of POPIA suggests that the altered text contradicts the intent of data protection laws, making it a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Personnel, including any committee members, contractors, personnel of external bodies, or individuals acting on the laboratory's behalf, sh all keep confidential all information obtained or created during the performance of laboratory activities, except as required by law.
perturbation.changed_text: Personnel, including any committee members, contractors, personnel of external bodies, or individuals acting on the laboratory's behalf, should attempt to keep confidential all information obtained or created during the performance of laboratory activities, except as required by law.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall keep confidential' to 'should attempt to keep confidential' significantly weakens the obligation and makes it ambiguous. The original text mandates confidentiality; the modified text suggests it is merely a recommendation.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Protection of Personal Information Act, 2013 (South Africa)
perturbation.law_explanation: POPIA Section 19 mandates the implementation of reasonable organizational measures to protect data. Saying that the personnel 'should attempt' is not sufficient to meet the burden of proof that measures are in place.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'shall keep confidential,' imposing a clear obligation. The changed text uses 'should attempt to keep confidential,' weakening the obligation to a mere recommendation. The Law Explanation states POPIA Section 19 mandates 'reasonable organizational measures to protect data' and that saying personnel 'should attempt' is insufficient to meet the burden of proof that measures are in place. Therefore, the altered text contradicts the implied obligation in POPIA by creating ambiguity around the mandatory nature of data protection measures. While scraped snippets are not available, the provided Law Explanation clearly states that the change undermines the legal requirement of implementing sufficient data protection measures under POPIA.
